str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - programming-resources
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: Strategies for Learning to Code with Limited Resources
entry.summary: In today's digital age, learning to code has become a crucial skill for many individuals looking to advance their careers or pursue their entrepreneurial dreams. However, for those who lack access to expensive resources or coding bootcamps, it can seem like an insurmountable challenge. In this blog post, we explore ten strategies that can help aspiring coders learn the basics of programming with limited resources. From utilizing free online resources and joining online communities to practicing regularly, collaborating with others, seeking mentorship, and working on real-world projects, these tips provide a roadmap for anyone who wants to take their first steps into the world of coding. By following these strategies, you can develop practical skills that will prepare you for future opportunities in the tech industry, regardless of your location or budget. So, whether you're a beginner or an experienced coder looking to sharpen your skills, this post is for you! Join us as we explore the world of coding and learn how to overcome common barriers to entry in the digital age. Together, let's inspire the next generation of tech innovators!

entry.title: Overcoming the Challenges of Learning Multiple Programming Languages
entry.summary: In today's ever-evolving technology landscape, learning multiple programming languages has become a necessity for developers looking to stay relevant and competitive in their respective industries. However, this process can be daunting and challenging, especially for beginners or those with limited experience. This blog post aims to provide ten practical points that can help overcome these challenges and make the language-learning journey more manageable and enjoyable. From setting realistic goals and prioritizing languages to using multiple resources, practicing consistently, collaborating with others, understanding concepts before moving on, reviewing previous learning, staying motivated, and being patient, this post provides insights and tips that can help readers become more versatile developers in the long run. By sharing these ideas and encouraging our readers to share their experiences, we hope to foster a supportive community of developers who are passionate about expanding their skillset and advancing their careers.
